notes: National electricity grid operator (UNEG). Quasi-sovereign under Samruk-Kazyna. IPO'd on KASE 2014. Operates 25,000+ km of high-voltage transmission across Kazakhstan plus central dispatch.

segments.name: Transmission tariffs (regulated)
segments.description: Regulated tariff for use of national grid by generators and large consumers

segments.name: Dispatch and system services
segments.description: Real-time balancing and frequency control via CDC dispatch center

segments.name: Balancing electricity market
segments.description: Settlements for imbalance between scheduled and actual flows

segments.name: Cross-border trade fees
segments.description: Wheeling and transit fees for KZ-RU, KZ-UZ, KZ-KG cross-border flows

competitors.name: Distributed generation (rooftop solar, captive)
competitors.notes: Growing but small share; pressure on regulated tariff base

competitors.name: Russian grid (UES)
competitors.notes: Synchronously connected; import/export partner; political risk

competitors.name: CASA-1000 corridor (Central Asia - South Asia)
competitors.notes: New transmission corridor bypasses KEGOC for some flows

competitors.name: Renewables IPP captive transmission
competitors.notes: Future risk if IPPs build private high-voltage lines

prior_transformations.name: SAP ERP implementation
prior_transformations.description: Replace 1990s-era custom systems with SAP across HQ and regional MES units
prior_transformations.learnings: Customisation for Kazakhstan-specific tariff rules ate 60% of timeline

prior_transformations.name: AMR/AMI smart metering pilot
prior_transformations.description: Pilot rollout of smart meters at large industrial consumers
prior_transformations.learnings: Tech worked; commercial use case for KEGOC unclear since meters belong to distribution companies

prior_transformations.name: Dispatch system modernization (SCADA upgrade)
prior_transformations.description: Replace Soviet-era dispatch SCADA with modern EMS at CDC
prior_transformations.learnings: On track but timeline stretched by sanctions affecting Siemens/Schneider supply

prior_transformations.name: KASE IPO governance reforms
prior_transformations.description: Public listing forced board independence, IFRS reporting, investor relations
prior_transformations.learnings: Disclosure discipline raised execution quality across the org

strategic_priorities: Smart grid technologies for grid stability
ESG reporting for international borrowings (EBRD, ADB)
pain_points: Aging infrastructure - 60%+ of substations from Soviet era
Regulated tariff structure limits ROI on investment
Renewables intermittency causes balancing market volatility
Engineer talent flowing to private sector and abroad
Sanctions-driven supply chain risk on Western equipment
